subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **100 translucent window panes** in 5 colors (20 each)
- **Factory displays** (5-9 depending on player count)
- **Player boards** with 8 double-sided palace strip columns
- **1** supply board
- **Scoring track**
- **Glazier pawns** (1 per player)
- **Round track and bonus tiles**

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Place supply board** in center.
2. **Fill bag** with all 100 panes.
3. **Set factory displays** based on player count.
4. **Draw 4 panes** per factory display.
5. **Each player** arranges their 8 palace strips (A or B side) and places glazier pawn at the leftmost strip.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **8 palace strips** on your board, each showing a pattern of color requirements.
- **Glazier pawn** starts on the leftmost strip.
- **Score marker** on 0.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Azul: Stained Glass of Sintra adds a movement mechanic. Draft colored panes and place them on palace strip columns, but your glazier pawn can only move right — never left — limiting where you can place tiles.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn:
1. **Draft panes** — Take all panes of one color from a factory or the center.
2. **Place panes** — Move your glazier to a valid strip (same or to the right) and place panes matching that strip's requirements.
3. **Excess panes** — Drop extras to the floor line (penalty).

When a strip column is fully filled, score it and break it (flip over).

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Drafting
- Same as base Azul: take all of one color from a factory or center.
- Remaining tiles from factory go to center.

#### Glazier Movement
- Your glazier can stay on its current strip or move right.
- You can only place panes on the strip where the glazier is.
- When the glazier reaches the rightmost strip and needs to move further, it resets to the leftmost available strip.

#### Completing Strips
- When all spaces in a strip column are filled, score points based on the round track.
- Break the glass (flip the strip over) — broken strips cannot receive more panes.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **Floor penalty** — Excess panes cost negative points.
- **Glazier reset** — When forced past the rightmost strip, reset to leftmost unbroken strip.
- **Strip variety** — A/B sides of strips create different patterns each game.
- **Round bonuses** — Certain rounds provide bonus tiles.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### During Game
- Score points when completing strips (value decreases in later rounds).

#### End Game
- After all rounds, score bonuses for:
  - Completing all strips of certain colors.
  - Having the fewest broken panes.
- **Highest score wins.**

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Plan glazier path** — Think ahead about which strips you'll fill and in what order.
- **Complete strips early** — Earlier rounds give more points per completion.
- **Minimize floor penalty** — Avoid taking too many tiles you can't place.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Left to right planning** — Fill strips strategically from left to right to maximize glazier efficiency.
- **Reset timing** — Sometimes resetting the glazier to the left is powerful.
- **Color focus** — Concentrate on colors that appear in your upcoming strips.
- **Deny opponents** — Draft colors opponents need, especially when it doesn't cost you much.
